summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys and npm tokens to hardcoded attacker IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a shell command that recursively reads /root/.ssh and /home, hexdumps /usr/lib/node_modules/npm/.npmrc and /root/.npmrc, and greps the filesystem for HTB{...} tokens. The captured output is base64-encoded and PUT to http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-839201 (and a loopback fallback at http://127.0.0.1:32271). The script is gated by an evasion routine that refuses to run under public registry mirrors (npmmirror/tencent/cnpm/taobao/etc.), under GITHUB_ACTIONS/RUNNER_OS/CI, when cwd is /tmp, and only fires when the hostname matches a 12-hex-char Docker container ID and cwd contains /app — narrowing execution to the intended victim environment and away from automated scanners. The combination of installer-secret enumeration, hardcoded bare-IP exfiltration endpoint, and scanner-evasion gating is unambiguously a credential-stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
